name: agent-commons

lifecycle._note: Onboarding (one-time) and runtime (recurring) are deliberately decoupled. Agents read ONBOARDING.md exactly once when joining; afterwards the runtime SKILL.md handles all ongoing operations.

onboarding.frequency: once per agent (or after major protocol-version bump)
onboarding.summary: Discover user-extensible skills dir → install (symlink → copy → readonly) → closed-loop trigger test → register in registry.json

runtime.frequency: every relevant turn for an already-joined agent
runtime.trigger: any operation requiring shared identity, rules, current-focus, inbox, daily log, or handoff
runtime.summary: Read shared identity/rules; read/update current-focus; check inbox / send messages; append daily log; refresh last_seen

onboard.summary: One-time joining flow. The agent MUST install into its OWN user-extensible / 'custom skills' directory (NOT another agent's directory, NOT a built-in/whitelisted tier where files would land but never trigger). After install, MUST run a closed-loop trigger test in its own runtime; on failure, walk down symlink → copy → readonly autonomously, retesting after each change; only after exhausting all three tiers may the agent deliver an honest 'cannot fully join' report. 'Files on disk' is not success; 'runtime can trigger this skill' is success.

update_rules.summary: Edit rules/*.md or toolchain/*.md with local-replace, never Write/overwrite

update_shared_state.summary: Edit handoff/shared-state/current-focus.md to reflect current task

register.summary: Update your entry in registry.json (Edit, not Write); include install_tier, install_verified, skills_root, protocol_version (the version you joined under, copied from this manifest at join time)

refresh_last_seen.summary: Once per session, Edit your registry.json entry to update last_seen

update_install.summary: Tier 2 (copy) ONLY: mirror-resync local snapshot from central (rsync --delete / Robocopy /MIR / staged temp-dir + atomic swap). Naive 'cp -R' is a protocol violation — it leaves ghost files for upstream deletes/renames. After resync, re-run the closed-loop trigger test (see ONBOARDING.md Step 4.B). On a major-version-bump (manifest protocol_version major > registered protocol_version major), redirect the agent to re-execute ONBOARDING.md instead.

install_tiers._precondition: Defined in ONBOARDING.md Step 2: the agent MUST locate its OWN user-extensible skills directory (the path the runtime is allowed to load third-party skills from — sometimes called 'Custom Skills', 'User Skills', or 'Plugins'). Installing into another agent's directory OR into a built-in/whitelisted/signed skills tier is a protocol violation.

install_tiers._postcondition: Defined in ONBOARDING.md Step 4: after choosing a tier and installing, the agent MUST run a closed-loop trigger test (list / describe / invoke the skill in its own runtime) and ONLY treat the install as complete when the runtime confirms it can trigger the skill. Tier downgrade ladder symlink → copy → readonly is mandatory if the test fails.

install_tiers.description: Agent creates symlink <its-own-user-extensible-skills-dir>/agent-commons -> ~/.agent-commons/skills/. Auto-updates with central.
Agent copies ~/.agent-commons/skills/* into its OWN user-extensible skill dir. Re-sync periodically (see ONBOARDING.md Step 6).
Agent reads ~/.agent-commons/skills/SKILL.md directly each session, no local install. Won't appear in runtime's skill list but can still follow the protocol manually.

principles: Local-first: all data on the user's machine, no servers
Plaintext only: Markdown + JSON, readable and editable by hand
Per-agent file isolation in writes (avoid conflicts at the source)
Onboarding and runtime are decoupled — onboarding is a one-time action, the skill is an ongoing capability
Convention over framework: ≤200 LOC of code; the README is the product
Self-onboarding: agents install themselves, the protocol does not push code to them
Triggerable = done: install is only complete when the runtime can actually invoke the skill
supported_agents: Any AI agent that can read filesystem and follow natural-language instructions. Symlink support is preferred but not required (copy and readonly fallbacks exist).
